strengths: 20-year engineering career spanning embedded systems, high-throughput data infrastructure (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic), cloud (AWS CDK at Optilyz), and current AI/agentic tooling — comfortably exceeds the 15-year floor with rare cross-domain breadth.

Hands-on AI builder credentials: currently authoring MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq, directly satisfying the preferred 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI' qualification.

Sustained entrepreneurial and community-leadership track record — founding and maintaining GISCollective since 2016, delivering conference talks (FOSS4G), and running full-day public workshops — maps squarely to the role's 'inspire others internally and externally' responsibility.
concerns: Holds a BS in Computer Science with no advanced degree; the role explicitly prefers a Master's or PhD in an AI-related field.
Current title is Staff Software Engineer, a step below the Director-level IC expectation; the resume shows no evidence of advising strategic enterprise customers or partners at scale.

AI-specific experience is nascent (~14 months at re:cinq); prior roles are predominantly implementation-focused rather than AI platform strategy or technical thought leadership at an organizational level.
response.justification: Bogdan presents a compelling technical foundation: over two decades of engineering across embedded systems, real-time high-throughput infrastructure, cloud platforms, and now active construction of MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — precisely the 'builder fluency in AI' the role prizes most. His entrepreneurial persistence with GISCollective and his proven ability to teach and inspire through public workshops and conference talks demonstrate that his impact already extends beyond individual code contributions. The central question is one of trajectory rather than capability: the leap from Staff Engineer to Director-level IC advising strategic customers is material, and his AI work, while directly on-point, is still in its early chapters. For an organization willing to invest in that acceleration, Bogdan offers a rare combination of genuine hands-on AI product experience and the long engineering pedigree to contextualize it — making him a strong candidate for a deeper technical conversation.
